revenue: H1 2025 total revenue was $90.84 billion (up slightly YoY from $90.58 billion); Q2 2025 specific revenue is obscured in the excerpt formatting.
net_income: Q2 2025 net loss of $29 million, down significantly YoY from a net income of $1.83 billion in Q2 2024.
key_metrics: Q2 2025 Operating Income: $511 million (down from $1.88 billion in Q2 2024)
Inventories: $17.27 billion (up from $14.95 billion at Dec 31, 2024)
qoq_changes: Net income swung to a $29 million loss in Q2 2025.
Inventories increased by $2.32 billion since year-end 2024.
Short-term debt (excluding Ford Credit) more than doubled to $3.59 billion from $1.76 billion at year-end.
new_risks: Significant deterioration in profitability and operating margins.
Working capital pressure from rising inventory levels.
Increased reliance on short-term debt for the core automotive business.
management_tone: Cautious, reflecting significant margin compression, a swing to a net loss, and rising inventory levels despite stable top-line performance in the first half.
analyst_note: Ford's Q2 2025 results highlight severe profitability headwinds, swinging to a net loss of $29 million alongside a sharp drop in operating income. Rising inventories and a doubling of short-term automotive debt suggest working capital challenges that require immediate management intervention.
